summary: Round 12 reopens HNSW from the round-11 hard stop rather than broadening the rejected filtered fallback line. Task 16 produced a new promoted local signal around shared_bitset_batch4: on the deterministic bitset diagnosis lane, shared grouped batch-4 distance improved qps by about +15.68% to +17.81% versus the preserved Task-14 baseline while recall stayed at 0.9942 and visited/frontier/distance counters remained unchanged. The new tracked hypothesis is shared_bitset_batch4: carry this grouped shared bitset layer-0 batch-4 mechanism onto the authority same-schema lane and verify whether it creates any real remote evidence without changing the archived HNSW family verdict in advance.
action: Keep benchmark_results/hnsw_p3_002_final_verdict.json unchanged, freeze the round-11 authority hard stop plus the promoted Task-16 local screen evidence as the round-12 baseline, and require a fresh authority rerun before making any new HNSW performance claim.
